step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{C}
**Clinical Reasoning:** Wide divergent roots in primary teeth, similar to those seen in developmental anomalies like taurodontism or bifid roots, can complicate both endodontic and extraction procedures. These configurations increase the risk of root fracture during extraction due to uneven stress distribution along the root axis—especially affecting the apical portion—and may also contribute to residual apical portions being unresorbed during natural healing processes, making them clinically detectable via radiograph. Thus, both issues described in choices A and B are supported by the evidence related to root morphology and extraction complications.
